title: Approaches to tenancy in Postgres
description: There are many ways to slice a Postgres database for multi-tenant applications. Let's look at the three most common approaches and the trade-offs.

title: Keeping a Postgres queue healthy
description: Dead tuples from high-churn job queues can silently degrade your Postgres database when vacuum falls behind—especially alongside competing workloads. Traffic Control keeps cleanup on track.

title: Patterns for Postgres Traffic Control
description: Practical patterns for leveraging Database traffic Control

title: Graceful degradation in Postgres
description: Not all traffic is created equal.When a database is overwhelmed, you want the important queries to keep executing, even if that means shedding lower-priority work.This is a much better outcome than the alternative: a total database outage.

title: High memory usage in Postgres is good, actually
description: A high memory percentage in PlanetScale Postgres is not necessarily a problem. Let's compare how memory and CPU usage are different, how not all memory usage is created equal, and which signals actually require attention.

title: Stripe Projects partnership: Provision PlanetScale Postgres and MySQL databases from the Stripe CLI
description: PlanetScale is a co-design and launch partner for the Stripe Projects developer preview, allowing you or your coding agents to provision and manage databases and other dev tools directly from the Stripe CLI.

title: Enhanced tagging in Postgres Query Insights
description: Introducing query tagging improvements in Postgres Query Insights

title: Behind the scenes: How Database Traffic Control works
description: Learn how Traffic Control enforces real-time limits on Postgres queries.

title: Introducing Database Traffic Control
description: Enforce real-time limits on your Postgres query traffic to protect your database from runaway queries and unexpected load spikes.

title: Scaling Postgres connections with PgBouncer
description: PgBouncer is the perfect pairing for Postgres's biggest weakness: connection management. Tuning it just right is important to make this work well, and here we cover everything you need to know
